children.value: Base on the CNRS AI Rising Talents application (
), but the shape is fundamentally different — short-essay form, not a dossier. See
for the full call: 5 textareas + CV + 2 references, no word limits.
AI/CS committee evaluating a cosmologist’s research programme. Lead with cosmology, motivate it as the proving ground for agentic science, position AI as the practice that makes cosmology better.
AI researchers evaluating a domain expert who already lives inside Claude Code. They know agents deeply; they don’t know weak lensing. Lead with
the cosmologist who is already deep in real, daily, frontier Claude-Code-mediated science
— domain expertise + lived agentic practice as the offering.
Lived Claude Code practice at unusual depth.

UNIONS B-mode workflow (~10k lines agent-written, 120k total), felt, shuttle, lightcone — all real, all in production, all defensible. Question 3 (Claude Code usage) is where most applicants will be thin and Cail can be deep.
PhD in a hard STEM field with first-author frontier results.
SPT-3G CMB lensing, UNIONS B-mode lead author, SPT × Euclid MoU first author, h-index 19, ~1,640 citations.
A view on what “doing science with AI” actually means
— agentic science framing, tapestry as verification infrastructure, the bitter-lesson scaffolding with the verification counter-theme. Cail has thought about this carefully and at length.
Sketches per question — entry shape, draw-from, length target. To shape with Cail; nothing here is committed until he reads it.
“Please share the STEM field(s) where you have the most expertise and describe your relevant experience/contribution.”
opener (the LCDM-and-systematics paragraphs that survived the close-read pass), compressed to ~3 short paragraphs.
observational cosmology, specifically the percent-precision systematics regime where the field now lives.

(1) what cosmology is converging toward and where the open questions are, (2) Cail’s specific corner — CMB lensing with SPT-3G, cosmic shear with UNIONS, Euclid×CMB cross-correlations, kinematic lensing supervision; the throughline is

children.html: B

children.value: -mode paper, SPT-3G lensing pipeline, SPT × Euclid MoU first author, bibliometrics line. Voice: arrive through content; first sentence is about the universe, not about Cail.
“Describe any experience you have with ML/AI, e.g. training custom models, applying an existing model, using adjacent techniques, etc.”
Cail is a domain scientist, not an ML researcher. Name that cleanly.
The small ML usage that is real and distinctively his.
word2vec for a Foucault philosophy project (2018) + text-to-speech models run locally. Dates the timeline as someone curious about these tools years before the LLM party; texture, not padding.
The statistical substrate of his actual work.
Bayesian inference, MCMC, hierarchical modeling, likelihood design, posterior validation (AU Mic, UNIONS, SPT-3G). Then the soft on-ramp:
the more substantive recent AI experience has been applying frontier LLMs as collaborators in scientific workflows — see Q3.
Brevity is the signal. Most applicants will pad Q2 by reaching; Cail being short and pointing forward distinguishes him.
“Describe any experience you have with Claude Code or similar agentic coding products. If you have used them before, please describe your use case(s) and workflow.”
Where Cail wins or loses this application.
The shape, with Lightcone now forward:

children.html: B

children.value: -mode paper (Daley et al. 2026), conducted almost entirely through Claude Code: ~10k lines of agent-written analysis code, figures, manuscript; ~120k lines edited across the workflow. Peer-reviewed; presented at Moriond; the role-shift (architect rather than implementer); since mid-2025 Cail has worked this way exclusively.
Lightcone Research as joint CNRS–Berkeley open-source project building
the open ecosystem for reproducible, composable, and verifiable scientific research in the age of agentic AI
(Go CLI he maintains, tracks decisions/findings as structured fibers),
(open YAML schema for analysis nodes carrying decisions + alternatives + evidence anchors),
(viewer that renders an analysis as browsable provenance graph).
“Lightcone is the infrastructure AI co-scientists are missing” — public framing, complement-not-competitor to Claude Code. Lets us wave at the value-prop in
Fiber → Claude Code (surveys, drafts against constitution, reports at handoffs) → shuttle (cross-session handoff to fresh agent against same constitution) → verification. Posture:
human as architect, agent as implementer
; structured record makes review tractable at this volume.
“This application is being co-drafted with Claude” — embodies Anthropic’s own transparency principle. Charming if it lands, easily cuttable if it doesn’t.
the open ecosystem I work inside
. Larry Page funding mentioned in voice; left out of the written draft (private, and CNRS+Berkeley is sufficient public framing). Researcher verbs.
the agentic-science section of CNRS 5pp, reshaped — flip from “selling agentic science to AI evaluators” to “telling AI evaluators how I already do this.” Reader knows agents; speak peer-to-peer.
Longest of the five. Still well under a dossier section.
“Propose one or two concrete ideas for evaluating, improving, or applying Claude in your field that you think are promising and would be excited about.”
Non-binding. Anthropic frames this as a
signal: mentors co-design the actual project. So the bar is not “this is what we should do” but “look at how I think about leveraged uses of Claude in real science.” Lead with
, since the question invites both axes (“evaluating, improving, or applying”).
Candidates, ranked by my read of fit (Cail to reshape):

children.html: B

children.value: -mode validation, CMB lensing pipelines, kinematic lensing) as evaluation harnesses for Claude, scored by numerical agreement + expert rubric + LLM-as-judge. The existing AI-science benchmarks evaluate isolated tasks; this would evaluate the

— data → analysis → judging the result’s trustworthiness. Initial release possible inside 3 months given Cail’s existing analyses. Aligned with Anthropic’s own move toward evaluation grounded in messy real problems (OpenAI’s Proof Q&A is the closest precedent on the model-lab side).
Cail is uniquely positioned; Anthropic doesn’t have this; deliverable inside the fellowship; useful to Anthropic past the fellowship.
Provenance/verification as a first-class capability inside long-running Claude sessions.

When a Claude Code session produces a multi-step scientific analysis, automatically emit an inspectable provenance graph (ASTRA-shaped: nodes for decisions, jobs, findings; edges for data flow; evidence anchors) that humans or downstream Claude instances can audit. This is a
: does the model produce reasoning that another instance can trustworthily verify?
uses the tapestry/felt work Cail has already built; the science-verification thread maps onto a broader Claude capability concern (auditable agentic work).
more infrastructure-shaped; needs framing that lands with Anthropic as model-capability research, not as tooling.

Use Claude to systematically critique published scientific claims — trace chains of reasoning, identify load-bearing assumptions, flag missing checks. Evaluate against historical cases where errors were eventually caught (BICEP2 dust, OPERA neutrinos) and current open tensions. Connects to the PleIAs scientific-claim-verification thread.
less “build something inside Claude,” more “use Claude to verify”; possibly too applied for Anthropic’s interest.
I will build evaluation that grounds your models in real science, and I will push on the model-side capability that makes scientific Claude trustworthy.
Both inside three months. Both leveraged by Cail’s existing work.
To check with Cail before drafting.
“Provide two references that can speak to your past work and experience.”
— CNRS, CosmoStat, Lightcone Research co-lead. Speaks to agentic-science practice from inside; daily proximity to Cail’s Claude Code work.
— Brand & Monica Fortner Endowed Chair in Theoretical Astrophysics, UIUC. Cail’s PhD advisor; 5 yrs close supervision on SPT-3G CMB lensing. Certifies STEM foundations.
Only attachment. Carries weight. The CNRS CV at
is the first Research subsection, supervision and outreach are present, bibliometrics are there. Two options:
as a standalone PDF, lightly retargeted (remove dossier-specific framing if any; tighten Research-Interests bullets toward AI/agentic-coding). Fast, defensible, already polished.
that pushes agentic-coding practice up and to the front. More effort; possibly redundant with what Q3 already says.
The CV’s job is to certify the STEM record; Q3 carries the Claude Code story.
; open on the universe / the practice / the claim.
No declarative slogans, no triplet punchlines, no empty signposting.

Textarea register: tighter than dossier prose, but still flowing — these are short essays, not bullet lists. Bullets sparingly, where they earn their keep (e.g., distinguishing the parts of the agentic workflow in Q3).

Per Cail (May 12 chat): Lightcone is much bigger than the prior architecture had it. Joint CNRS–Berkeley open-source project funded by Larry Page (private; don’t write); going public this week; premier talk at Uros Seljak’s festival; six-person team with Cail fourth on the linear list; three advisors; developer summit at the end of June (during the fellowship window). Liam (Berkeley PhD with Seljak, possible Dario Amodei adjacency) is on the team. The North Star document explicitly positions Lightcone as
— public framing that lands hot with Anthropic and is

Lightcone is forward in Q3 (substrate Cail contributes to) and provides backdrop for Q4 (project ideas land inside an ecosystem Anthropic can see, not in a vacuum). CV gets a clean Lightcone line in the agentic-science section.
— Cail’s own load-bearing identity claim (“I want to remain a scientist, not a builder or businessman”). Lightcone is
the ecosystem I contribute to as a scientist

Liam may meet with Dario Amodei. Whatever Anthropic sees from us should not be discordant with what arrives in Dario’s awareness through Liam’s channel. Possibly worth a check-in with Liam on framing before submission.
Confirmed: in-person SF + US work auth + June 15 – Sept 15.
(Cail to confirm logistics and citizenship status.)
Concur with benchmarks + provenance-as-capability (now Lightcone-anchored), or different lean?
Reuse CNRS CV as standalone, or build leaner version with Lightcone forward?
Four days. Suggest: today — Q3 draft (showpiece) + Q1 draft; tomorrow — Q2 + Q4; Thursday — close-read + cohesion + CV export; Friday — submit early morning.
honest + texture (word2vec/Foucault, Bayesian substrate, soft on-ramp to Q3).
voice only, implicit. The beach-with-friends future animates the writing from behind; not explicitly stated.
I would like to apply to the Anthropic STEM Fellowship, https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/anthropic/jobs/5189848008
We can base the application strongly on my cnrs ai rising talents application, although i want to put a

of effort into it this week since it’s a hugely high-reward application. i think first step is to open a new sub-fiber with the full application requirements, including all the questions on the application with word limits, etc. then you can stay open and not kill ppid or close, and i’ll come chat and we’ll start fleshing out a plan and application

frontmatter.name: anthropic-stem-fellowship
frontmatter.outcome:
**Substantial close-read pass on Q1 + Q2 + Q3 ¶1 landed (May 14); drafts now plain-text and paste-ready for Greenhouse.** Q1 rewritten across all three paragraphs — umbrella opener (weak gravitational lensing), chronological PhD→postdoc arc with "difficult reality of cosmological data" framing and 3–6-year analysis foreshadow, CEA Paris-Saclay credentials cluster with CNRS glossed at first mention. Q2 restructured into 2 paragraphs + handoff — ML knowledge + Bayesian/frequentist substrate + bias-vs-variance argument; word2vec/Foucault final project with ultimate-point sentence + TTS/STT + Pleias reframed for synthetic-data scaling. Q3 ¶1 added orienting sentence, replaced "results" with "null-test validation," added arXiv link inline, rewrote role-shift around "constructing back pressure that allows the agent to check its own work." CV: in-prep language replaced with "submitted to A&A"; publication counts synced at 4 further refereed. All markdown stripped from Q1–Q5 prose. **References (Lanusse + Holder) confirmed — Cail has been in touch.**

**Remaining for Cail:** close-read on Q3 ¶2–4 (Lightcone substrate, day-to-day workflow, multi-language tools), Q4 (three project ideas + Anthropic motivation + France–Anthropic closer), Q5 (reference context paragraphs). CV interactive walk-through still parked at [[application/anthropic-stem-fellowship/cv-rebuild]] (header shape, Lightcone wording trim, CNRS phrasing, any 2026 additions, Skills "AI & workflows" line). Optional INSPIRE eyeball. **Paste into Greenhouse and submit by Friday May 15.**
frontmatter.title: anthropic-stem-fellowship
